ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 covers pear juice generically; no Korea-specific pear juice entry exists. Used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers pear juice generically; Korean pear (Pyrus pyrifolia) variety not distinguished at this level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for Korean pear juice identified; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Korean pear juice is approximately 200 ml (one small bottle or cup), as commonly sold at Korean restaurants and convenience outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Pear juice, canned or bottled, unsweetened — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 168175)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Korean pear (Pyrus pyrifolia / nashi pear) juice is not separately listed in USDA FoodData Central. USDA SR Legacy entry for unsweetened pear juice (FDC ID 168175) is used as the closest defensible analogue. Korean pear juice is typically slightly lower in sugar than European pear juice; values are treated as a reasonable upper-bound estimate. Sodium is confirmed low. Energy and macros are consistent with published Korean food composition data for pear-based beverages.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Korean pear juice (Pyrus pyrifolia) was identified in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 43 is derived from the published GI of pear juice (unsweetened) reported in Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables, which lists pear juice at GI 43 (glucose reference). Korean pear juice has a similar sugar profile (predominantly fructose and glucose) and is considered a defensible analogue. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (43 × 22.8) / 100 = 9.8, using 200 g serving with 11.4 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (4 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Korean pear juice is very low in sodium at approximately 4 mg per 100 g, making it a suitable beverage choice for individuals managing hypertension, provided it is consumed without added salt or high-sodium accompaniments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Natural fruit sugars (approximately 9.6 g sugar per 100 g) and moderate glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Korean pear juice contains approximately 9.6 g of naturally occurring sugars per 100 g and has a low GI of 43; however, a 200 ml serving delivers approximately 19 g of sugar and a glycemic load of ~9.8, so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size and prefer whole pear over juice to benefit from fibre-mediated glucose attenuation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and low sodium; naturally occurring polyphenols in pear
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Korean pear juice is essentially fat-free and very low in sodium, and pear-derived beverages contain flavonoids and hydroxycinnamic acids that may support vascular health; it can be included as an occasional beverage in a heart-healthy diet when consumed in moderate portions without added sugars.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Korean Pear Juice (배즙, Baejup) is a lightly sweet, refreshing beverage made by pressing or juicing Korean pear (Pyrus pyrifolia), also known as nashi pear or Asian pear. The Korean pear is larger, rounder, and crisper than European pear varieties, with a high water content and a delicate, mildly sweet flavour profile dominated by fructose and glucose. The juice is typically minimally processed — pressed and filtered — and may be sold fresh, chilled, or in small sealed bottles. In Korean culinary tradition, pear juice and pear extract are also used as a natural meat tenderiser due to the enzyme actinidin, and the beverage is consumed both as a refreshing drink and as a digestive aid. In Singapore, Korean pear juice is served at Korean restaurants as a non-alcoholic accompaniment to bibimbap, Korean BBQ, and other Korean dishes. Nutritionally, a 200 ml serving provides approximately 92 kcal, 22.8 g carbohydrate, and 19.2 g natural sugars, with negligible fat and protein. The glycemic index is estimated at 43 (low GI), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate at approximately 9.8 due to the volume of natural sugars. Sodium content is very low at approximately 4 mg per 100 g, making it suitable for low-sodium diets. Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate intake due to the natural sugar content. The beverage is vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal-suitable.

meal_context: children's drink at Korean family restaurants

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Korean pear juice (Pyrus pyrifolia) was found in a primary regional source such as HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy unsweetened pear juice (FDC ID 168175), which is the closest defensible analogue in an approved source. GI is estimated from the Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables using pear juice as an analogue. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 168175 (pear juice, canned or bottled, unsweetened) as no direct Korean pear juice entry exists in approved primary sources. Korean pear (Pyrus pyrifolia) juice is compositionally similar but may have slightly lower sugar content than European pear juice; values represent a reasonable upper-bound estimate. 2. GI of 43 is an analogous estimate based on pear juice GI from Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for Korean pear juice was identified. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for pear juice. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for pear juice. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for pear juice. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for pear juice. 7. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for unsweetened pear juice). trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — naturally zero as this is a plant-derived fruit juice). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero as this is a plant-derived beverage with no animal-derived ingredients). 8. Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names are not applicable for this Korean-cuisine beverage and are left null per multilingual rules (Korean dishes require Korean only). 9. The input food_name '梨饮料 Korean Pear Juice' has been normalised to canonical English 'Korean Pear Juice'; the Chinese form '梨饮料' is preserved in aliases.
